description: delete (stop) a realtime alert rule on Alert Bridge by rule ID (DELETE /api/v1/realtime/<id>) — Workflow D
rules: DESTRUCTIVE — deletes a running rule and stops its VLM stream
the skill's user-facing yes/no confirmation gate (state rule ID + sensor, get an explicit 'yes') MUST be satisfied by the caller BEFORE this op; this perk performs the already-confirmed deletion
that confirmation ALWAYS applies — including under autonomous / pre-authorized / CI execution; a 'run autonomously' instruction does NOT authorize skipping it
RULE_ID is the rule UUID returned by subscribe-create or subscribe-list — resolve it first; do not guess
HTTP 404 means the rule is already gone ('no longer active — nothing to stop'); 502 rtvi_vlm_unavailable means the stream stop failed
VLM real-time mode only; Alert Bridge base is http://<HOST_IP>:9080; never route through the VSS Agent /generate
usage: Set HOST_IP and RULE_ID (the rule UUID). Optional: ALERT_BRIDGE_URL (override base http://<HOST_IP>:9080). The porter issues the DELETE and records the HTTP status + response as one JSON audit line. Output: subscribe_stop.json.
limitation: Requires the VSS alerts profile deployed in VLM real-time mode with Alert Bridge reachable at http://<HOST_IP>:9080. Offline / unreachable Alert Bridge degrades gracefully (records http_code=000) without deleting anything.
